finding.contract_claim: [AST] L96: function `JunkAnalyzer.analyze` ‚Äî No cross-file references found (AST-proven)
finding.triage_reasoning: The grep for `.analyze(` across the entire repo returns zero real call sites ‚Äî only string literals in manifest fixtures and adjudication notes that document this same gap. The orchestrator in `audit.py` (L728‚Äì742) constructs each `JunkAnalyzer` subclass and dispatches exclusively through `analyzer.analyze_async(...)`. No subclass overrides `analyze`; all override `analyze_async`. The `analyze` method (L96‚Äì115) is a concrete sync wrapper on the ABC that has been orphaned by the orchestrator's evolution to async dispatch. All three predicates hold: (1) Reality ‚Äî the gap exists; `.analyze()` is never invoked. (2) Significance ‚Äî dead API surface on a public ABC misleads subclass authors and creates a false impression that a sync entry point is supported. (3) Actionability ‚Äî remove the `analyze` method from `JunkAnalyzer`.
finding.suggested_fix: Delete the `analyze` method (L96‚Äì115) from `JunkAnalyzer` in `src/osoji/junk.py`. If a sync entry point is genuinely needed in the future, it should be re-introduced deliberately and wired into the orchestrator.
